taxonomy: Chamomilla recutita, commonly known as German chamomile, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is classified within the genus Matricaria, which is a group of daisy-like plants. The species recutita is further distinguished from other related species within the genus, such as Matricaria chamomilla.
identification: Chamomilla recutita, commonly known as German chamomile, can be identified by its daisy-like flowers with white petals and yellow centers, which are typically 1-2 cm in diameter. The leaves of the plant are fern-like, bipinnate, and have a distinctive apple-like scent when crushed. Chamomilla recutita can be distinguished from other plants in the same genus by its annual growth habit and lack of hair on the lower leaves.
uses: Chamomilla recutita is used to heal a variety of ailments, including insomnia and restlessness, which it helps to alleviate through its calming effects on the mind and body. It also has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing swelling. Chamomilla recutita has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and stomach ulcers, by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Furthermore, it has been used to alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties make it a popular remedy for allergies and skin irritations, such as eczema and acne.
benefits: Chamomilla recutita is used to alleviate a wide range of health issues, from soothing digestive problems and calming anxiety to reducing inflammation and promoting better sleep. Its medicinal uses include treating skin irritations, wounds, and menstrual cramps, as well as providing relief from headaches, nausea, and allergies. The health benefits of chamomilla recutita are numerous, and it has been traditionally used to support eye health, lower blood pressure, and even ease symptoms of PMS. Additionally, its calming effects have made it a popular natural remedy for promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving overall well-being.
properties: Chamomilla recutita has many therapeutic properties, such as its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, making it an effective remedy for soothing irritated skin and joints. Additionally, this plant exhibits antispasmodic properties, allowing it to calm muscle spasms and provide relief from menstrual cramps and other gastrointestinal issues. The antioxidant properties of chamomilla recutita help to protect the body from oxidative stress, promoting overall well-being and supporting the immune system. Its antibacterial properties also make it an effective natural remedy for wound care and preventing the growth of bacteria. Furthermore, chamomilla recutita's anxiolytic properties provide a calming effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety levels, promoting relaxation and better sleep quality.
constituents: Chamomilla recutita has been found to contain a wealth of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The plant also contains terpenoids, including chamazulene, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. Additionally, Chamomilla recutita contains tannins, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and essential oils, including alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. These bioactive constituents work synergistically to provide a range of health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
parts: The most commonly used parts of Chamomilla recutita are its flowers, leaves, and roots. The flowers are primarily used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, often found in teas, ointments, and salves to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate anxiety. The leaves, on the other hand, are utilized in herbal teas to promote digestion, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support liver function. Meanwhile, the roots are often employed in traditional medicine to treat insomnia, stomach issues, and as a natural antibacterial agent to combat infections.
preparations: Chamomilla recutita most common herbal preparations are used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate anxiety. Infusions, also known as teas, are commonly used to calm restlessness and insomnia, as well as to treat menstrual cramps and headaches. Topical applications like creams and ointments are used to relieve skin irritations, such as eczema and acne, while also providing relief from muscle and joint pain. Tinctures, essential oils, and capsules are often used to treat anxiety, stress, and restlessness, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
side_effects: Chamomilla recutita side effects are diverse and can range from mild to severe. Individuals with allergies or sensitive skin, such as young children, pregnant women, and people with eczema or psoriasis, are most likely to experience side effects, which can include skin irritation, redness, itching, and rashes. Other potential side effects include dizziness, headaches, nausea, stomach upset, diarrhea, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of bleeding in individuals taking anticoagulant medications. Precautions also include avoiding the use of chamomilla recutita in people with bleeding disorders, as it can exacerbate bleeding tendencies and interact with medications such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and sedatives.
title: chamomilla recutita
intro: Chamomilla recutita, also known as German chamomile, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anti-anxiety effects, which make it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Chamomilla recutita include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and soothing digestive issues, as well as alleviating symptoms of menstrual cramps and insomnia. Chamomilla recutita can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, capsules, and essential oil, which can be used internally or topically to reap its therapeutic benefits.
actions: Chamomilla recutita has anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and nervine properties, which exert therapeutic actions on the musculoskeletal system, nervous system, and gastrointestinal system. It also possesses antimicrobial, anti-anxiety, and antioxidant properties, affecting the immune system, endocrine system, and cardiovascular system. Additionally, Chamomilla recutita has anti-emetic, anti-allergic, and anti-exudative properties, impacting the respiratory system and integumentary system. Furthermore, its antiseptic, antipruritic, and soothing properties influence the skin and mucous membranes.
intro_study: According to African Journal of Traditional, Complementary, and Alternative Medicines (AJTCAM), Chamomilla recutita was found to have a higher prevalence and was used by ancestral healers in the Chimborazo province of Ecuador to cure different illnesses.
